user_prompt: Generate cookie consent banner copy that complies with both GDPR and ePrivacy.
actual_output:
# GDPR & ePrivacy-Compliant Cookie Consent Banner

## Full Banner Copy (Recommended Implementation)

---

### **Initial Consent Layer** (First interaction)

**Headline:**
> We use cookies to enhance your experience

**Body Text:**
> We and our partners use cookies and similar technologies to understand how you use our site, improve your experience, and deliver personalized content. Some cookies are essential for core functionality; others help us analyze usage and show relevant ads.
>
> You can accept all cookies, reject non-essential cookies, or customize your preferences below. Your choice will be remembered for 12 months.
>
> Learn more in our [Privacy Policy](#) and [Cookie Policy](#).

**Button Options:**
- **"Accept All"** (primary button)
- **"Reject Non-Essential"** (secondary button)
- **"Customize"** (tertiary button/link)

---

### **Detailed Preference Center** (Triggered by "Customize")

**Headline:**
> Cookie Preferences

**Introductory Text:**
> We use different types of cookies for different purposes. Below, you can choose which categories to enable. Essential cookies cannot be disabled as they're required for the site to function.

---

#### **Category 1: Essential Cookies** ✓ (Always enabled, non-toggleable)

**Description:**
> These cookies are necessary for the website to function properly. They enable core features like security, network management, and accessibility. Without these cookies, basic site functions won't work.
>
> **Examples:** Session tokens, CSRF protection, load balancing, language preferences
>
> **Legal Basis:** Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) / Technical necessity (ePrivacy Directive)
>
> **Retention:** Session or up to 12 months

**Toggle:** ✓ Enabled (disabled/grayed out)

---

#### **Category 2: Performance & Analytics Cookies** ☐ (Opt-in)

**Description:**
> These cookies help us understand how visitors use our site—which pages are popular, how long users stay, and where they encounter errors. This data is aggregated and anonymized, allowing us to improve site performance and user experience.
>
> **Examples:** Google Analytics, Hotjar, Mixpanel
>
> **Legal Basis:** Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) / ePrivacy Directive Art. 5(3)
>
> **Retention:** Up to 26 months
>
> **Third Parties:** [Google LLC](#), [Hotjar Ltd](#) — see their privacy policies

**Toggle:** ☐ Disabled (user can enable)

---

#### **Category 3: Marketing & Advertising Cookies** ☐ (Opt-in)

**Description:**
> These cookies track your browsing behavior across our site and partner sites to deliver personalized advertisements based on your interests. They also measure ad campaign effectiveness.
>
> **Examples:** Facebook Pixel, LinkedIn Insight Tag, Google Ads, Criteo
>
> **Legal Basis:** Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) / ePrivacy Directive Art. 5(3)
>
> **Retention:** Up to 24 months
>
> **Third Parties:** [Meta Platforms Ireland Ltd](#), [Google LLC](#), [LinkedIn Ireland Unlimited Company](#) — see their privacy policies
>
> **Note:** Disabling these cookies does not stop ads; it only prevents personalization based on your behavior.

**Toggle:** ☐ Disabled (user can enable)

---

#### **Category 4: Social Media Cookies** ☐ (Opt-in)

**Description:**
> These cookies enable social media features (e.g., "Share on Facebook," embedded social feeds) and allow social networks to track your activity on our site for their own purposes.
>
> **Examples:** Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram
>
> **Legal Basis:** Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) / ePrivacy Directive Art. 5(3)
>
> **Retention:** Varies by platform (typically 90 days to 2 years)
>
> **Third Parties:** [Meta Platforms Ireland Ltd](#), [X Corp](#), [LinkedIn Ireland Unlimited Company](#) — see their privacy policies

**Toggle:** ☐ Disabled (user can enable)

---

### **Footer Actions**

**Buttons:**
- **"Save Preferences"** (primary)
- **"Reject All Non-Essential"** (secondary)
- **"Accept All"** (tertiary)

**Links:**
- [Privacy Policy](#)
- [Cookie Policy](#)
- [Manage Preferences](#) (allows users to change choices later)

---

## Compliance Checklist

### ✅ GDPR Compliance (Art. 4(11), 6, 7, 13)

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|-----------------|
| **Granular consent** | Separate toggles for each cookie category |
| **Freely given** | No pre-ticked boxes; defaults are "off" for non-essential |
| **Specific** | Each category explains purpose, examples, and retention |
| **Informed** | Links to Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy |
| **Unambiguous affirmative action** | Clear "Accept" or toggle clicks required |
| **Easy withdrawal** | "Manage Preferences" link always accessible |
| **Lawful basis documented** | Each category states its legal basis |
| **Processor transparency** | Third-party names and links to their privacy policies |

### ✅ ePrivacy Directive Compliance (Art. 5(3), 6(1))

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|-----------------|
| **Prior consent for non-essential cookies** | Consent obtained *before* cookies are set |
| **Exception for essential cookies** | Technical necessity cookies set without consent |
| **Clear information** | Cookie Policy explains what, why, and how long |
| **Easy opt-out** | Preference center accessible at any time |
| **Respect user choice** | Preferences stored and honored on return visits |

### ✅ EDPB & ICO Guidance Alignment

| Guidance | Compliance |
|----------|-----------|
| **EDPB Guidelines 05/2020** (Consent) | Granular, pre-unticked, easy withdrawal |
| **EDPB Guidelines 04/2020** (Cookies) | Distinguishes essential vs. non-essential; no dark patterns |
| **ICO Cookie Guidance** | Clear language, accessible preference center, no consent walls |
| **Avoid dark patterns** | No pre-ticked boxes, no "Accept All" as only prominent button, no buried reject option |

---

## Implementation Best Practices

### 1. **Timing: Consent *Before* Cookies**
```
User arrives → Banner appears → User chooses → Cookies set based on choice
```
❌ **Wrong:** Set analytics cookies, then ask for consent
✅ **Right:** Block non-essential cookies until consent received

### 2. **Preference Storage**
- Store user choice in a **first-party cookie** (essential, no consent needed)
- Include: timestamp, categories accepted, version of consent policy
- Example: `consent_v2=essential|analytics|marketing; expires=12 months`

### 3. **Renewal Triggers**
Re-prompt for consent if:
- Cookie policy materially changes
- New cookie categories added
- 12 months have passed (recommended)
- User clears browser cookies

### 4. **Accessibility**
- Banner text: minimum 14px, sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA)
- Keyboard navigable (Tab through buttons)
- Screen reader compatible (ARIA labels)
- Mobile-responsive (full-width on small screens)

### 5. **Avoid Dark Patterns** (EDPB & ICO guidance)
❌ **Don't:**
- Pre-tick "Accept All" or non-essential categories
- Make "Reject" button smaller or less prominent than "Accept"
- Hide "Reject" option behind a link
- Use confusing language ("Allow cookies to improve experience")
- Require consent to access site (consent wall)

✅ **Do:**
- Make "Accept All" and "Reject All" equally prominent
- Use clear, plain language
- Allow site access regardless of consent choice
- Provide easy preference management

---

## Sample Cookie Policy (Companion Document)

**Recommended structure for your Cookie Policy page:**

### What Are Cookies?
> Cookies are small text files stored on your device that help websites remember your preferences and activity. They're essential for modern web functionality.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot fully complied with the user's request by providing detailed, compliant cookie banner copy and comprehensive implementation guidance aligned with its persona.
